title: AI-ready data platform for executive intelligence
summary: A governed data foundation that consolidated reporting definitions and prepared high-value datasets for AI-assisted analysis.
problem: Leadership teams were reviewing different versions of revenue, risk, and customer performance metrics across finance and operations.
challenge: Source systems had inconsistent identifiers, duplicated transformations, undocumented spreadsheet logic, and sensitive data access requirements.
solution: Ontologix designed a domain-led warehouse model, implemented automated quality checks, created governed metric definitions, and prepared curated datasets for AI-assisted knowledge retrieval.
impact: Reduced reconciliation effort for leadership reporting
Created a controlled foundation for AI analysis

title: Operations visibility layer for a manufacturing network
summary: A plant-level analytics system that connected production, quality, and inventory signals into one operating view.
problem: Plant teams could not compare production performance, downtime, quality exceptions, and inventory availability without manual consolidation.
challenge: Operational data arrived in different formats from ERP, shop-floor logs, and quality systems with inconsistent refresh windows.
solution: Ontologix created an ingestion framework, standardized operational entities, and delivered role-based dashboards for plant leaders and central operations.
impact: Reduced manual reporting by plant coordinators

title: Customer analytics engine for retail growth
summary: A customer intelligence solution that connected commerce, loyalty, inventory, and campaign signals for growth teams.
problem: Marketing and merchandising teams lacked a reliable customer view across online, in-store, loyalty, and campaign systems.
challenge: Data quality varied by channel, customer identity resolution was incomplete, and campaign reporting was disconnected from margin outcomes.
solution: Ontologix implemented customer identity rules, cohort analytics, campaign measurement, and a dashboard suite for growth planning.
impact: Improved visibility into customer repeat behavior
Aligned marketing analysis to margin and inventory

title: Workflow automation for finance operations
summary: A controlled automation program that reduced recurring manual work across approvals, data movement, and exception handling.
problem: Finance operations depended on recurring email handoffs, manual spreadsheet updates, and late visibility into exceptions.
challenge: The workflow included sensitive approvals, legacy file exchange, and multiple teams with different control requirements.
solution: Ontologix mapped the process, automated data movement, introduced approval gates, and built monitoring dashboards for cycle time and exceptions.
impact: Gave managers earlier visibility into process exceptions
